channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: ⚖️ Iran releases prominent human rights lawyer Nasrin Sotoudeh on bail
item.description: Iran freed prominent human rights lawyer Nasrin Sotoudeh, who was arrested in April during a crackdown amid the conflict with the US and Israel. Her release on bail marks a rare concession during wartime.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Russia places former UK Defence Minister Ben Wallace on wanted list
item.description: Russia has placed former British defence minister Ben Wallace on its wanted list in connection with an unspecified criminal investigation, escalating tensions with the UK.

item.title: 🇹🇷🇦🇲 Turkey and Armenia move closer to direct trade links
item.description: Turkey lifted some customs restrictions on Armenia, paving the way for direct trade between the two countries in a further sign of normalizing ties after more than 30 years of strained relations.

item.title: 📈 US producer prices surge most in four years as Iran war drives inflation
item.description: US producer prices posted their biggest increase in four years in April, boosted by soaring costs for goods and services, the latest sign of accelerating inflation amid the war with Iran.

item.title: 🇭🇺 Hungary summons Russian ambassador over drone strikes on western Ukraine
item.description: Hungary summoned Russia's ambassador after unprecedented drone attacks hit western Ukraine near its border. The strikes came hours after PM Orban criticized EU support for Ukraine.

item.title: ⚔️ Russian Forces Ordered to Behead Ukrainian Soldiers, Ukraine General Staff Reports
item.description: Ukraines General Staff reports that Russian forces were ordered to behead and display the bodies of two fallen Ukrainian soldiers, calling it a deliberate violation of the rules of war.

item.title: 🇭🇺 Hungary Summons Russian Ambassador After Zakarpattia Drone Attack
item.description: Hungary summoned Russias ambassador after Ukraines western Zakarpattia Oblast, home to a Hungarian minority, was targeted in Russias mass drone attack that killed at least six people.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇵🇱 US Army Abruptly Cancels Poland Deployment, Europe Personnel at Pre-2022 Levels
item.description: The US Army has abruptly halted a planned deployment of 4,000 soldiers to Poland, bringing American personnel numbers in Europe to pre-2022 levels before Russias invasion of Ukraine.

item.title: 🛩️ Air Force MQ-9 Reaper Fleet Drops to 135 Aircraft After Iran Combat Losses
item.description: The US Air Force is seeking to backfill its MQ-9 Reaper inventory after combat losses in the Iran conflict, while fielding a cheaper, more expendable replacement drone.

item.title: 🇦🇹 Austrian Jets Intercept Unauthorized US Military Aircraft Two Days in a Row
item.description: Austrian fighter jets intercepted US military aircraft flying through Austrian airspace without authorization on consecutive days, highlighting tensions over military flight corridors.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russia Launches Massive Drone Barrage on Western Ukraine, Killing Six
item.description: Russia fired approximately 800 drones at about 20 Ukrainian regions in a daylight attack targeting critical infrastructure near NATO borders, killing at least six people and prompting Poland to scramble fighter jets.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 Trump Arrives in Beijing for High-Stakes Summit with Xi Jinping
item.description: President Trump arrived in China with a delegation including Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang and Elon Musk for a summit focused on trade, AI competition, and the Iran war. The visit comes amid tensions over Taiwan arms sales and rare earth controls.

item.title: 🏛️ US Senate Blocks Bid to Restrain Trump Iran War Powers as Support Grows
item.description: Senate Republicans blocked the latest Democratic effort to end the Iran war without congressional authorization, but a third Republican voted to advance the bill, signaling growing bipartisan concern over the conflict.

item.title: 🛢️ IEA Warns Global Oil Supply Will Fall Below Demand Due to Iran War
item.description: The International Energy Agency revised its outlook, warning that global oil supply will fall short of demand this year as the Iran war disrupts Middle East production and drains inventories at an unprecedented pace.

item.title: ⚖️ House Lawmakers Reach Threshold to Force Vote on Ukraine Aid Package
item.description: A discharge petition to force a House vote on Ukraine security aid and new Russia sanctions reached 218 signatures, the latest successful bid by lawmakers to defy Republican leadership.

item.title: 🏦 ECB to hike rates in June as Iran war fuels inflation spike
item.description: The European Central Bank will raise interest rates next month and at least once more this year to combat energy price shocks from the Middle East war, according to a Reuters poll of economists.

item.title: 🇮🇳 India races to shield economy from Iran war-driven oil shock
item.description: A sustained spike in energy prices from the Iran war has clouded India macroeconomic outlook, spurring crisis-era measures to protect Asia third-largest economy.

item.title: 🛡️ NATO leaders call for stronger eastern flank air defense after Russian breaches
item.description: Leaders of 14 NATO allies said Russia repeated airspace violations underline the urgent need to consolidate alliance air defenses against missiles and drones.

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU warns of risks from growing reliance on US gas
item.description: European energy regulators warned that the EU increasing dependence on U.S. gas carries risks, including rising reliance on a single supplier.

item.title: ⚓🇺🇸 US Navy Could Run Out of Money by July, Top Officer Warns
item.description: Chief of Naval Operations Adm. Daryl Caudle told lawmakers the Navy might have to modify operations if Congress does not pass a budget, highlighting funding concerns amid ongoing global deployments.

item.title: 🇮🇱🗳️ Netanyahu Coalition Submits Bill for Early Elections in Israel
item.description: Israel's ruling coalition moved to dissolve parliament, potentially triggering snap elections amid domestic political pressures during the ongoing Iran conflict.

item.title: 🇨🇺🇺🇸🇨🇳 US Renews $100M Cuba Aid Offer as Trump Raises Island with Xi
item.description: Washington offered renewed humanitarian assistance to Cuba while Trump discussed the island nation in talks with Chinese leadership, highlighting competing influence efforts in the Caribbean.

item.title: 🇨🇳⚡🌍 Iran War Driving Global Shift Toward Chinese Clean Energy
item.description: The conflict with Iran is accelerating countries' transition to Chinese EVs, solar panels and batteries, strengthening Beijing's position ahead of Trump-Xi trade negotiations.

item.title: ⛽ Ukraine Resumes Strikes on Russian Energy Infrastructure After Ceasefire Ends
item.description: Ukraine renewed attacks on Russian oil refineries and ports after a US-brokered ceasefire expired. The escalation comes as peace talks stall and both sides intensify energy infrastructure targeting.

item.title: 🌍 How the Iran War Has Reshaped Middle East Alliances and Economies
item.description: A comprehensive analysis of how the US-Israeli bombing campaign that began February 28 has destabilized the region, damaged infrastructure and economies, and overturned assumptions about regional security.
